U0777.name: Data Validation Abend
U0777.description: Job failed due to invalid data in a record. This typically occurs when data does not match the expected format defined in the copybook or when validation rules are violated.
common_causes: Data format mismatch with copybook definition
Missing or extra fields in record
resolution_steps: Isolate the problematic record (check error message for record number)
Compare record structure with copybook definition
Verify data types match field definitions
Check for invalid characters or formatting issues
Fix the data in the input file
Re-run the failed step
prevention: Add data quality checks in upstream processes
Keep copybooks synchronized with data sources

S013.name: Dataset Open Error
S013.description: Job failed to open a dataset due to DCB (Data Control Block) attribute mismatch. This commonly occurs when RECFM (Record Format) or LRECL (Logical Record Length) in JCL doesn't match the actual dataset attributes.
common_causes: RECFM mismatch between JCL and dataset
LRECL mismatch between JCL and dataset
Dataset not cataloged or not found
resolution_steps: Check the DD statement in JCL for the failing DDNAME
Verify RECFM and LRECL values in JCL
Compare with actual dataset attributes using ISPF 3.4 or LISTCAT
Correct the JCL DD statement to match dataset attributes
If dataset is new, ensure it's allocated with correct attributes
Re-run the job
prevention: Use standard DCB attributes across the organization
Use JCL procedures with predefined DCB parameters

S0C7.name: Numeric Data Exception
S0C7.description: Program attempted to perform arithmetic operation on invalid numeric data. This occurs when a field defined as numeric contains non-numeric characters or is not properly initialized.
common_causes: Character data moved to numeric field
resolution_steps: Identify the field causing the exception (check program dump)
Examine the data in the problematic field
Remove or replace invalid characters
Verify field is properly initialized before use
Check if data conversion is needed
Validate input data before processing
Re-run the job
prevention: Add numeric validation before arithmetic operations
Implement data quality checks on input files
Use NUMVAL or NUMVAL-C for safe conversions

S0C4.name: Protection Exception / Storage Violation
S0C4.description: Program attempted to access storage outside its allocated region or violated storage protection. This is a serious error indicating a program logic problem.
resolution_steps: Review program dump to identify failing instruction
Check array subscripts and table references
Verify working storage definitions
Look for uninitialized pointers or addresses
Review recent program changes
Add boundary checking in the program
Recompile and test in development environment

S0C1.name: Operation Exception
S0C1.description: Program attempted to execute an invalid operation code. This usually indicates program corruption or incorrect program load.
resolution_steps: Verify correct program load module is being used
Check program compilation and link-edit steps
Recompile and relink the program
Verify program library is not corrupted
Check for storage overlays in calling programs
Review recent program deployments
prevention: Maintain separate development and production libraries

S322.name: Time-Out / Job Exceeded Time Limit
S322.description: Job exceeded the maximum CPU time or elapsed time specified in the JCL TIME parameter.
resolution_steps: Review program logic for infinite loops
Check if data volume has increased significantly
Analyze program performance and optimize if needed
Increase TIME parameter in JCL if appropriate
Check for database locks or contention
Review system resource availability
prevention: Set appropriate TIME parameters based on historical data
Monitor and optimize program performance regularly
Use efficient algorithms and data structures

S806.name: Program Not Found
S806.description: System could not find the program specified in the EXEC statement. The program may not exist in the specified library or the library concatenation is incorrect.
common_causes: Program not in STEPLIB or system library
Incorrect program name in EXEC statement
resolution_steps: Verify program name spelling in EXEC statement
Check STEPLIB DD statement includes correct libraries
Verify program exists in specified library using ISPF 3.4
Check library concatenation order
Ensure program has been compiled and linked
Verify user has authorization to access the library

S222.name: Job Cancelled by Operator
S222.description: Job was cancelled by the system operator or automated monitoring tool, usually due to excessive resource usage or operational requirements.
resolution_steps: Contact operations team to understand cancellation reason
Review job logs for resource usage
Check if job can be optimized
Reschedule job if it was cancelled for maintenance
Adjust resource parameters if needed
Coordinate with operations for rerun
prevention: Schedule resource-intensive jobs during off-peak hours
Communicate with operations about critical jobs
Implement proper job monitoring and alerting
